name: Winsock Service Provider / LSP
categoryDescription: Registry keys that cause code to execute at boot, logon, or via Windows subsystem hooks.
description: Winsock protocol and namespace catalogs — Layered Service Providers (LSPs) registered here intercept all network traffic at the socket layer.
forensic_value: LSPs are a powerful network interception mechanism — a malicious LSP sees all unencrypted network traffic from all applications. Once common in adware/spyware; now rare but still valid. Any unexpected provider DLL is suspicious.
